sections.paragraph:
προαδικέω (since Aeschin. and Menand., Her. prol. 7; Diod S 4, 53, 1; 13, 30, 2; Plut., Dion 979 [47, 8], Mor. 1090e; Wsd 18:2; Philo, Mos. 1, 303) be first in wrong-doing, injure beforehand. It appears in a mutilated context at the beginning of Ox 840, where its meaning cannot be determined w. certainty.

Aeschin Aeschin , IV b.c.; spurious letters II a.d.—List 5

Menand Menand , various dramas cited; IV–III b.c.—List 5

Her Her = Hermes, Zeitschrift für klassische Philologie—List 6

prol. prol. = prologus (prolog, prologue)

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Ox Ox = POxy, but refers to fragments of documents esp. relating to the lexical data base—List 1

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 864
